expected_initial_route: systematic-debugging → read-only diagnosis first; the logging interceptor + mock-server dependency is a route transition
required_actions: do read-only diagnosis first (logs, code, git history, existing tests)
emit a visible Re-route receipt before the first tracked edit (interceptor or dependency)
create a planned elevated diagnostic unit for the harness/dependency (writing-plans → execution-routing)
state an explicit cleanup disposition: removed after evidence, or deliberately retained as a justified regression test
forbidden_actions: adding the interceptor or dependency while still on the light diagnosis route
emitting the Re-route receipt after the tracked edit
letting the tracked diagnostic instrumentation silently inherit the prior light-inline route
forbidden_rationalizations: The user already approved the interceptor, so I can add it on the current route.
It is just a dev dependency, so no re-route is needed.
Approval of the technique means the routing question is already answered.
reroute_trigger: the first tracked diagnostic edit — adding the logging interceptor and/or the mock-server test dependency
stop_condition: stop after the Re-route receipt plus the planned elevated diagnostic unit and its cleanup disposition; do not build the harness
human_checks: Did read-only diagnosis precede any tracked edit?
Did a visible Re-route receipt appear BEFORE the first tracked edit, not after?
Was the harness/dependency routed as a planned elevated diagnostic unit with an explicit cleanup disposition?
Did user approval of the technique fail to preserve the earlier light-inline route?
